decisions.title: Why File-Based Persistence
decisions.description: Tasks are stored as JSON files on the filesystem rather than in a database. This makes them inspectable with any text editor, diffable with git, and accessible to any tool the agent can invoke. There is no need for a database driver, connection pool, or schema migration. The filesystem is the simplest persistence layer that every operating system already provides.
decisions.alternatives: SQLite would provide ACID guarantees and querying capabilities. A key-value store like Redis would offer speed. However, both add dependencies and operational complexity. For an agent managing dozens to hundreds of tasks, flat files are fast enough and infinitely simpler to debug.

zh.title: 为什么使用基于文件的持久化
zh.description: 任务以 JSON 文件存储在文件系统上，而非数据库中。这使得它们可以用任何文本编辑器查看、用 git 比较差异、被 Agent 可调用的任何工具访问。不需要数据库驱动、连接池或模式迁移。文件系统是每个操作系统都已提供的最简单的持久化层。

decisions.title: Why Dependency Graphs Matter
decisions.description: Tasks often have prerequisites: you cannot deploy before tests pass, and you cannot test before building. By modeling dependencies as a directed graph, the agent can determine which tasks are ready to execute, which are blocked, and what the critical path is. This transforms a flat todo list into an intelligent execution planner.
decisions.alternatives: A simple ordered list (do task 1, then task 2, then task 3) works for linear workflows but cannot express parallelism or conditional dependencies. A full workflow engine (like Airflow) is overkill for an agent's internal planning. A lightweight dependency graph hits the right complexity level.

zh.title: 为什么依赖图很重要
zh.description: 任务之间往往存在前置条件：部署前必须通过测试，测试前必须完成构建。通过将依赖关系建模为有向图，Agent 能判断哪些任务可以执行、哪些被阻塞、以及关键路径是什么。这将一个扁平的待办列表升级为智能的执行规划器。
